title: camphorosma monspeliaca subsp. lessingii
intro: Camphorosma monspeliaca subsp. lessingii, also known as the saltwort or lesser saltwort, is a plant species belonging to the Amaranthaceae family. It is primarily found in arid and semi-arid regions, characterized by its succulent leaves and stems, and ability to tolerate high salt concentrations. Traditionally, Camphorosma monspeliaca subsp. lessingii has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's health benefits include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, with herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and salves being used to harness its medicinal properties.
